Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets understand the normative process 1 Always starts with Receive order 2 Next is Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment are made 3 If inventory sufficient Receive Check Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Authorize payment after Check inventory before packaging Package items after authorization before shipping Ship order after packaging 4 If insufficient Receive Check Cancel order Close order no fulfillment after cancellation Key constraints extracted Receive order first Check inventory before Authorize payment Package items Ship order Cancel order Authorize payment after Check inventory before packaging Package items after authorization before shipping Ship order after packaging never after cancellation Close order final

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 09:00 Receive order 10:00 Check inventory 11:00 Authorize payment 12:00 Package items 13:00 Ship order 14:00 Close order Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This matches the sufficient inventory path exactly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 09:00 Receive order 09:30 Check inventory 10:00 Package items 10:30 Authorize payment 11:00 Ship order 11:30 Close order Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Violations Package items occurs BEFORE Authorize payment The normative description says Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment Package items So Case 2 violates the ordering packaging before payment authorization

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 09:00 Receive order 09:20 Check inventory 09:40 Authorize payment 10:00 Cancel order 10:30 Ship order 11:00 Close order Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Violations Authorize payment occurs but then the order is canceled if the inventory check reveals insufficient after checking inventory directly proceed to Cancel order here they authorized payment before canceling logically if insufficient should not authorize payment Ship order occurs after Cancel order explicitly prohibited Ship order without prior Package items Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Also existence of both Authorize payment and Cancel order suggests mixed path normative text presents mutually exclusive paths

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 4 09:00 Receive order 09:10 Check inventory 09:20 Cancel order 09:50 Close order Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close This matches the insufficient inventory path exactly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 09:00 Receive order 10:00 Check inventory 10:30 Authorize payment 11:00 Ship order 11:30 Close order Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Violations Missing Package items Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment Package items Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Therefore shipping without packaging is a violation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me double-check Case 3 Is there any ambiguity about whether Authorize payment could happen before a cancel The text says for insufficient inventory after checking the inventory the process should directly proceed to Cancel order Authorize payment is between Check and Cancel violates direct progression Authorizing payment and then canceling suggests either inventory check wrong or changed mind but normative frames distinct paths even if lenient on Authorize then Cancel the Ship after Cancel and Ship without Package are unambiguous violations Let me reconsider Case 2 Could Package items before Authorize payment ever be acceptable The text says Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared sets clear sequence Authorize Package Ship So Package before Authorize is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Are there any other subtle issues In Case 3 Close order is final Yes In Case 5 Close order is final Yes Is Check inventory immediately after Receive The description After an order is received the next logical step is to check In all cases Check inventory is second Good

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let me formulate the answer Violating Cases 2 3 and 5 Case 2 Violation Package items occurs before Authorize payment reversing required sequence Case 3 Violation 1 Ship order after Cancel order illogical to continue fulfillment after cancel Violation 2 Ship order without prior Package items Violation 3 Authorize payment before Cancel order violating direct Check Cancel and mixing branches Case 5 Violation Ship order without Package items skips mandatory step Case 1 and 4 compliant

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: The prompt says to interpret ambiguities as best I can For example one might question whether its ever acceptable to authorize payment before certain conditions fully confirmed or cancellation precede checks but description guides what is correct In Case 5 is packaging skippable if digital no context implies physical items inventory stock shipment In Case 3 maybe authorized then discovered fraud canceled but normative says cancellation by insufficient inventory so stick to described logic Another potential ambiguity Is Check inventory allowed non-immediate second but in all cases it is second no issue I think my analysis is solid
